text:
There once was a boy named Max. Max lived in the town of Cherryville. Every Sunday morning Max would walk past Mrs. Henderson’s garden. Mrs. Henderson kept rare birds. Max saved his pennies for weeks to buy featherberries.

One crisp autumn morning Max dashed into Mrs. Henderson’s garden. Everything was quieter than usual. Even the birds seemed wary.

"Oh dear," Mrs. Henderson whispered from behind an olive saxophone. "What an audacious young thing you are."

Max nodded nervously. "I just love these berries."

She handed him a plump berry. It was brown. Max took a bite. It was tasteless.

Then Mrs. Henderson said, "You wish!"

She plucked a worm from an orange leaf. Max caught it.

Mrs. Henderson plucked another worm. It was sticky.

"Oh dear again!"

She showed Max. It was full of ants.

"Ants? Really?" Max couldn’t believe his luck.

"Eating ants makes you wise
